title: anemonastrum rupestre subsp. rupestre
intro: Anemonastrum rupestre subsp. rupestre, also known as the alpine cleavers or alpine wood sorrel, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, and is said to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing. The herbal preparations of Anemonastrum rupestre subsp. rupestre include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes, and its use should be approached with caution.
